meta.setting: Clear Creek crossing near French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in hard travel and practical labor

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek mining travel and frontier settlement pressure.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek as homeland and life-giving water context; avoid turning water knowledge into spectacle.

image_prompt.subject: three ford men waist-deep at a cold Clear Creek crossing, guiding a frightened wagon team through brown water while riders wait on both banks
image_prompt.scene: overcast morning after snowmelt; a rope line tied to a cottonwood, wet horses snorting, muddy banks chewed by wagon wheels, pine slopes beyond, and a toll box kept above the water on a stump
image_prompt.composition: square image with diagonal creek current cutting the frame; the wagon is the central hazard, the ford men form a working triangle around the lead team, and waiting riders create social pressure at both edges
image_prompt.camera: low bank-level angle just above the waterline, early-HD spray, chunky silhouettes, and readable hand signals
material_cues: cold creek water against wool trousers
wet horse foam and dark harness leather
dull iron toll box with no readable markings
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Let the water look heavier than the people. The drama is in small hand signals, one wrong hoof placement, and the fact that everybody waiting has an opinion.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine action plate
render_traits: slightly simplified geometry with strong silhouettes
modest texture resolution on water, mud, wool, wood, and tack
hard ambient occlusion under wagon frame and horse bodies
baked gray daylight and cold creek reflection
believable 1899 clothing, wagon, harness, and tools
practical labor pose instead of heroic posing
style_modifiers.palette: cold creek blue, muddy umber, wet wool gray, pine green, raw wood tan, horsehide brown, and dull iron.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signage, labels, brands, or text. Any marks on the toll box should be indistinct scratches.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The ford men, wagon, horses, rope line, and current are all fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 frontier travel.
The image shows labor, danger, and negotiation rather than decorative scenery.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
